# Testing and measurement hypotheses

Use this file to make the research process disciplined instead of anecdotal.

| ID | Area / form | Hypothesis | Primary tactic | Best metric | First test |
|---:|---|---|---|---|---|
| H406 | Variant design | One major variable per test produces cleaner learning than changing hook, text, length and CTA simultaneously. | isolate variable | confidence in result | single-variable batch vs mixed batch |
| H407 | Variant design | A 3-variant test is usually enough to detect direction before producing a 10-variant batch. | small batch first | learning speed | 3 variant pilot then scale |
| H408 | Variant design | Reusing the same body with different first frames is the fastest way to test scroll-stop. | constant body | chose-to-view | 5 first frames, same body |
| H409 | Time windows | 24h metrics reveal packaging and early audience fit; 7d/30d metrics reveal search and durable value. | time-window diagnosis | 24h/7d/30d deltas | rank winners by each window |
| H410 | Time windows | A video that loses 24h but wins 30d should be classified as evergreen, not failed. | long-tail labelling | 30d views | feed winner vs search winner |
| H411 | Time windows | A video that wins 24h but dies by 7d should be treated as trend/emotion-led unless it also gains search traffic. | decay classification | view decay | spike-decay analysis |
| H412 | Cohort comparison | Compare videos within the same topic cluster, not across unrelated topics. | topic cohorting | fair metric comparison | caption videos vs caption videos only |
| H413 | Cohort comparison | Follower-heavy and non-follower-heavy posts need separate diagnosis. | audience segmentation | non-follower retention | all viewers vs non-followers |
| H414 | Cohort comparison | Small accounts should judge follow conversion and saves more heavily than vanity view totals. | quality metric weighting | follows/saves per view | views-ranked vs quality-ranked |
| H415 | Creative genome | Tagging first-frame type reveals repeatable patterns that raw view counts hide. | creative tagging | pattern discovery | 100-video genome |
| H416 | Creative genome | Winner mechanisms are more useful than winning topics. | mechanism extraction | repeat success | scale topic vs scale mechanism |
| H417 | Creative genome | Failed videos with strong saves can become stronger after packaging revision. | repackage utility | saves + low reach | repost with new first frame |
| H418 | Pre-post scoring | Videos scoring below threshold on first-frame clarity should be rebuilt before posting. | preflight rubric | chose-to-view | score vs performance correlation |
| H419 | Pre-post scoring | A video should declare its primary action before scripting. | action-led brief | target action rate | briefed action vs no action |
| H420 | Pre-post scoring | Scripts with no first payoff under 7 seconds should be considered high-risk for cold audiences. | payoff timing check | avg watch | payoff under/over 7s |
| H421 | Postmortems | Every postmortem should identify the failed gate, not just label the video good or bad. | gate diagnosis | next-test quality | generic notes vs gate notes |
| H422 | Postmortems | Comments should be coded as confusion, disagreement, identity, request or praise. | comment coding | content ideation | coded comments vs raw comments |
| H423 | Postmortems | Videos with high completion and low action need stronger save/send/follow design, not better hooks. | downstream diagnosis | action rates | change CTA/value not hook |
| H424 | Repurposing | Repurposed long clips need new native openings for each platform. | platform-native repurpose | chose-to-view | raw cross-post vs native open |
| H425 | Repurposing | The same insight can produce separate save, send and comment variants. | action variants | action-specific metrics | three endings/frames |
| H426 | Repurposing | Repurposing should preserve the mechanism, not the exact edit. | mechanism preservation | repeatability | copy edit vs copy mechanism |
| H427 | Series testing | A series should test one stable promise with variable examples. | stable premise + fresh examples | follows/return viewers | random videos vs series |
| H428 | Series testing | Series intros should be shorter as audience familiarity grows. | intro compression | retention over time | same intro vs shrinking intro |
| H429 | Series testing | A named series increases follow conversion only if the name communicates future value. | meaningful series label | follow rate | clever name vs clear name |
| H430 | Risk control | Controversy should be tested against trust metrics, not just comments. | trust-weighted controversy | follows/sentiment | neutral vs controversial |
| H431 | Risk control | Ragebait can be killed even if it wins comments when it loses completion or follow conversion. | kill rule | comments vs follows | comment-heavy loser analysis |
| H432 | Risk control | AI/template content should be audited for sameness before scaling volume. | originality audit | return viewers | template batch vs original batch |
| H433 | Decision rules | Scale when a mechanism wins at least two of: retention, save/send, follow, or 7d durability. | multi-metric rule | compound score | views-only vs compound score |
| H434 | Decision rules | Kill when a tactic improves starts but consistently damages completion and trust. | trust guardrail | completion/follows | clickbait guardrail |
| H435 | Decision rules | Iterate when the content value is strong but the packaging gate failed. | repackage rule | high saves/low reach | same body, new first frame/hook |
